slashCommands.name: /status
slashCommands.description: Show session info, model, usage, time

config.description: Anthropic API key for Claude models
OpenAI API key for GPT models
Google API key for Gemini models
Discord bot token for channel plugin
Telegram bot token for channel plugin

workflows.title: Multi-Channel Message Routing
workflows.description: Send same message to multiple channels simultaneously
steps: Configure channels in gateway config.yaml
Use `message({ action: "send", channel: "discord", target: "#general", message: "..." })`
Message routes through appropriate plugin
Can target specific channels, users, or threads

workflows.title: Agent Orchestration with Teams
workflows.description: Spawn parallel agents for different tasks
steps: Use `sessions_spawn()` to create subagents
Set mode to 'run' for one-shot or 'session' for persistent
Assign labels like 'builder', 'reviewer', etc
Use `sessions_send()` for inter-agent communication
Monitor with `sessions_list()`

workflows.title: Cron-Based Automations
workflows.description: Schedule tasks to run on a recurring basis
steps: Define cron jobs in config.yaml with schedule and task
Use cron syntax: '0 9 * * 1-5' for weekdays at 9am
Tasks are dispatched to the gateway on schedule
Results sent to configured channel (Discord, Telegram, etc)
Check HEARTBEAT.md for periodic health checks

workflows.title: Memory-Driven Agent Decisions
workflows.description: Use persistent memory to inform agent behavior
steps: Before answering, run `memory_search({ query: "topic" })`
Read relevant memory entries from MEMORY.md or daily logs
Use memory context in decision-making
After task, update memory with new facts/decisions
Keep entries concise for semantic search

workflows.title: Skill Extension via ClawHub
workflows.description: Install and manage skills to extend capabilities
steps: Run `clawhub search "keyword"` to find skills
Install with `clawhub install skill-name`
Update all skills: `clawhub sync`
Skills appear as commands in agent sessions
Check SKILL.md files to understand skill capabilities

proTips: Use model aliases to quickly switch between fast (Gemini) and quality (Opus) models
Set up daily Cron jobs to monitor GitHub activity, budget, or project status
Use memory_search before answering questions about prior work—don't rely on context alone
Configure heartbeat tasks in HEARTBEAT.md to keep agents responsive to incoming messages
Use MCP servers (filesystem, github, postgres) to extend agent capabilities
Set up Discord threads for agent conversations to keep chat organized
Use `--no-sandbox` carefully—only in controlled, trusted environments
Monitor agent logs with `openclaw logs` to debug issues
Create custom agent identities with SOUL.md for personality consistency
Use message reactions to gather user feedback on agent outputs
